summary: High-endemic areas are defined by annual incidence rates exceeding 10 cases per 100,000 people according to the WHO; this corresponds directly to option C provided. The focus here was clearly identifying and confirming these epidemiological thresholds set forth as indicators of heightened risk zones globally recognized through public health surveillance systems like those maintained by international organizations such as the WHO itself. To reiterate succinctly while adhering strictly within your requested constraints – it’s about meeting or surpassing an incident rate threshold which marks regions at higher susceptibility towards
